requestedDecision: Independently review the GH-16 change (dogfooding evaluation benchmark and routing calibration: new evaluation-manifest/evaluation-report/calibration-proposal v1 schemas, src/evaluation.ts pure aggregation and replay, eval-report/eval-replay/calibration-proposal CLI commands, synthetic fixtures, docs/evaluation.md) against the recorded contract acceptance criteria. Challenge: accepted-change denominators and missing-data counts, holdout/calibration separation (holdout never fits thresholds), over/under-routing signal definitions, relative-cost wording discipline (never an invoice), fail-closed parsing, and additive compatibility with existing schemas and commands. Report structured findings with severity; agreement is advisory and is not approval.
requiredActions: perAcceptedChange.elapsedMs divides by elapsedPresent, not accepted changes, contradicting its label and the documented denominator contract. Divide by accepted changes only when elapsed data is complete; otherwise return null and retain explicit present/missing counts.

Evidence is accepted after shallow schema-version checks, so malformed outcomes/attempts/plans can be aggregated with missing fields defaulted instead of counted as malformed. Parse artifacts with the existing strict parsers, count per-artifact validation failures without aborting, and add malformed-field fixtures.

When an attempt's relative cost is unknown, the report still emits a numeric partial total divided by all accepted changes, making unknown cost appear as zero or partial cost appear complete. Make configured-relative-cost totals/per-accepted-change values null or explicitly partial when contributing attempts are unresolved.

Calibration proposals accept arbitrary evidence task IDs and report hashes without manifest/split provenance, allowing calibration output to cite holdout results without a final-evaluation flag or refusal. Bind proposals to split-specific report/replay provenance and reject holdout task IDs for calibration proposals.

Replay marks a result changed only when candidate ID or status changes, so capability-class or configured-relative-cost changes under a stable candidate ID are summarized as unchanged. Compare the complete replay selection and add same-ID mutation tests.

The report schema is only an envelope: nested objects allow arbitrary properties, missingData has no defined fields, and counts/denominators/totals/nullable rates lack types. Fully specify all report fields with additionalProperties false and add negative schema tests.

Evidence reads follow symlinks without verifying containment within the selected evidence root, permitting a crafted evidence tree to read structured JSON outside that root. Resolve and validate root and per-task paths with realpath/lstat containment checks before reading.

Candidate aggregation keys only by model name, collapsing identical model names across providers or capability classes into one candidate. Use a composite identity such as provider/model/capability class.

The CLI input claims rigor.calibration-proposal.v1 but the schema requires generated artifactId/createdAt fields the checked-in proposal input omits. Add a distinct calibration-proposal-input schema or define and validate separate input/output variants.
